description: Implement the addressbook command with list/set/remove subcommands for managing named addresses scoped by chain ID, stored in .treb/addressbook.json, bringing the Rust CLI to full parity with the Go CLI on address management.

userStories.title: Addressbook Store Module
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an AddressbookStore in treb-registry with nested map structure (chain_id -> name -> address), CRUD methods, and Registry facade wiring so that addressbook data can be persisted in Go-compatible format.
acceptanceCriteria: New file crates/treb-registry/src/store/addressbook.rs with AddressbookStore struct following established store pattern (PathBuf + nested HashMap + load/save)
Internal data type: HashMap<String, HashMap<String, String>> — outer key is chain ID string, inner map is name→address
load() uses read_versioned_file() to accept both bare JSON and legacy wrapped format; returns empty map when file does not exist
save() converts to nested BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, String>> before calling write_versioned_file() for deterministic key ordering
CRUD methods: list_entries(chain_id) returning sorted Vec<(String, String)>, set_entry(chain_id, name, address), remove_entry(chain_id, name) with error on not found, has_entry(chain_id, name)
remove_entry cleans up empty chain maps (removes outer key when inner map becomes empty)
ADDRESSBOOK_FILE constant added to crates/treb-registry/src/lib.rs
Store module registered in crates/treb-registry/src/store/mod.rs with pub use
AddressbookStore field added to Registry struct with accessor methods (load_addressbook, addressbook, addressbook_mut, set_addressbook_entry, remove_addressbook_entry, list_addressbook_entries)
Registry::init() does NOT create an empty addressbook.json — file created on first write
Unit tests for store load/save round-trip, BTreeMap ordering, and empty-chain cleanup
cargo check -p treb-registry passes
cargo test -p treb-registry passes

userStories.title: Addressbook Command Scaffolding with Set Subcommand
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run treb addressbook set <name> <address> (or treb ab set) to add a named address entry scoped to the current chain ID so that I can manage addresses in my project.
acceptanceCriteria: New file crates/treb-cli/src/commands/addressbook.rs with AddressbookSubcommand enum (Set, Remove, List) and pub async fn run() dispatcher
Commands enum in main.rs gains Addressbook variant with #[command(alias = "ab")], --namespace/-s and --network/-n flags, and #[command(subcommand)]
Set subcommand accepts two required positional args: <name> and <address>
Address validation: must match ^0x[0-9a-fA-F]{40}$ with clear error message
Chain ID resolution: resolves --network value or config network to chain ID via Alloy Chain enum or numeric parsing; errors if no network configured
On success prints: Set <name> = <address> (chain <chainId>)
set calls Registry::set_addressbook_entry() then save()
Command registered in commands/mod.rs and dispatched in main() match arm
addressbook and ab mirrored in build.rs with all subcommands and flags
parse_cli_from unit tests: addressbook set and ab set parse correctly and identically
cargo check --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets passes

userStories.title: Addressbook Remove Subcommand
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run treb addressbook remove <name> to delete a named address entry from the addressbook for the current chain so that I can clean up unused entries.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove subcommand accepts one required positional arg: <name>
Resolves chain ID using the same utility as set (from --network or config)
Calls Registry::remove_addressbook_entry(chain_id, name) — errors if entry not found with message: addressbook entry '<name>' not found on chain <chainId>
On success prints: Removed <name> (chain <chainId>)
parse_cli_from unit test: ab remove Foo parses correctly
cargo check --workspace passes

userStories.title: Addressbook List Subcommand and Default Behavior
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run treb addressbook list (or treb ab ls) to see all named addresses for the current chain, with optional --json output and bare treb addressbook defaulting to list behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: List subcommand has #[command(alias = "ls")] for the ls shorthand
--json flag on List subcommand
Human output: entries sorted by name, each line %-24s %s with name in bold yellow and address in default color
Empty state: prints 'No addressbook entries found' (no error, exit 0)
JSON output: array of {"name": "...", "address": "..."} objects sorted by name via output::print_json()
Default subcommand: bare treb addressbook and treb ab default to list via argv normalization in normalize_cli_args(), keeping --help unnormalized
list alias ls mirrored in build.rs
parse_cli_from unit tests: bare addressbook normalizes to list, ab ls --json parses with json=true
cargo check --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets passes

userStories.title: Update Golden Tests, Help Snapshots, and Add Integration Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want golden help snapshots and integration tests for all addressbook subcommands so that behavior and output formatting are pinned against regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: New help_addressbook golden snapshot in integration_help.rs for treb addressbook --help
Root help golden (help_root) refreshed to include addressbook in Management Commands group

New integration test file crates/treb-cli/tests/integration_addressbook.rs with test cases: set_and_list, set_and_list_json, remove, remove_not_found, invalid_address, no_network, default_to_list, empty_list
Integration tests use TestContext with project fixture and --network flag with numeric chain ID
Tests use NO_COLOR=1 for stable human output assertions
UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test -p treb-cli --test integration_help refreshes help snapshots
All golden tests pass
cargo test -p treb-cli passes

userStories.title: CLI Compatibility Alias Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want alias parity tests verifying that addressbook vs ab and list vs ls produce byte-identical output so that backward-compatible aliases stay reliable.
acceptanceCriteria: New test cases in cli_compatibility_aliases.rs: addressbook_vs_ab_list, addressbook_list_vs_ls, addressbook_vs_ab_set, addressbook_default_vs_list
Each invocation uses a separate identically seeded temp project (since set/remove mutate state)
Tests follow established assert_matching_command_output pattern with seed function
cargo test -p treb-cli --test cli_compatibility_aliases passes

userStories.title: Go Compatibility Store Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Go-compatible addressbook fixture and store-level tests so that the Rust store can read Go-written addressbook data and produce Go-readable output without data loss.
acceptanceCriteria: New Go-compat fixture at crates/treb-registry/tests/fixtures/go-compat/addressbook.json with representative multi-chain bare JSON
Store-load test: AddressbookStore::load() correctly deserializes the Go-compat fixture
Round-trip test: load Go fixture → save → reload → verify entries unchanged
Key-set comparison: Rust-written JSON has identical recursive key structure to Go fixture
Confirm addressbook.json is bare JSON (not wrapped in _format/entries)
cargo test -p treb-registry passes
